3.happening: Create the empty map from signature to words.
3.why: Anagrams will share one signature key.

4.why: Each word gets its own frequency signature.
4.invariant: Words before this are already bucketed.

5.happening: Reset a 26-slot letter tally to zeros.
5.why: The tally will encode this word's letters.
5.invariant: Counts start empty for each word.

6.why: Every letter bumps its own slot.
6.invariant: The tally reflects letters seen so far.

7.happening: Map the character to a slot 0-25.
7.invariant: Every lowercase letter has a slot.

8.invariant: Slot totals equal letters counted so far.

9.happening: Freeze the counts into an immutable key.
9.why: A tuple can be used as a dictionary key.
9.invariant: Equal letter counts produce equal keys.

10.happening: Has this signature been seen before?
10.why: A new signature needs a fresh bucket.
10.invariant: Each distinct signature maps to one bucket.

11.happening: Open a new empty bucket for this signature.
11.why: It is the first word with these letters.
11.invariant: A bucket exists for every seen signature.

12.why: All words with these letters gather here.
12.invariant: A bucket holds only mutual anagrams.

13.happening: Return every bucket's list of words.
13.why: The buckets are the finished groups.
13.invariant: Every word sits in exactly one bucket.

init.why: Prepare to bucket words by letters.

loop.happening: Move to the next word or letter.
loop.why: Process every word and its characters.

update.happening: Count letters and bucket the word.
update.why: Build the signature, then file the word.

check.happening: Test whether the signature is new.
check.why: Decide to create or reuse a bucket.
